_comment: map_states_curated.json -- the hand-researched story layer for extract_map_states.py.
Keyed by pret script-file base name. Each stage: id (progression number; letters = branch),
script (the byte value the map RESTS at in this stage), events (ABSOLUTE list of pret flag
names SET in this stage -- everything owned-but-unlisted is CLEARED), context (subset of
flags that belong to OTHER maps' stories -- marked owned:false in the output; a context
name absent from `events` is recorded as explicitly cleared), missables (ABSOLUTE overrides
of this map's own missables -- unlisted ones sit at their defVal), badges (absolute),
transients (script values that are the cutscene LEAVING this stage, emitted as id N.k),
saveNotes (facts that cannot be expressed as bits -- cross-map toggles, derived tiles).
Every name is validated against events.json / missables.json / the map's own script table;
generation FAILS on anything unresolvable. Facts verified against pret/pokered 2026-07-17.

PalletTown.notes: The opening map. Its script byte rests at 0 only before Oak appears; the whole intro cutscene (values 1-4) runs once, and the byte then sits on 5 (the Daisy/Poke-Ball watcher) for the rest of the game. Value 6 (NOOP) is a fallthrough label the byte never actually holds. Daisy's sitting/walking swap writes Blues House's missables, not this map's.

stages.name: A quiet beginning
stages.desc: Fresh game. Oak has not appeared; stepping onto the tall grass row at the town's north edge is being watched for. Oak's street sprite is hidden.
stages.timeline: Game opening, before your first Pokemon.

trigger.text: New game -- every map script byte initialises to 0.

advance.text: Walk onto the north row (Y=1) toward Route 1: Oak shouts 'Hey! Wait!', walks to you, and leads you into his lab (cutscene values 1-4).

stages.name: Oak has led you to the lab
stages.desc: The intro cutscene has run: the byte now rests on the Daisy watcher (5) permanently. Oak's street sprite was shown for the cutscene and re-hidden by the follow-into-lab engine movement script (engine/overworld/auto_movement.asm).
stages.timeline: During/after the trip to Oak's Lab.

trigger.text: Stepped onto the north row without having followed Oak into the lab; the Oak cutscene ran to completion.

advance.text: Finish Oak's Lab: starter chosen, rival beaten, parcel delivered, Pokedex granted, and finally the Poke Balls received from Oak.

saveNotes: Oak's street sprite is re-hidden by the engine, not by this map's script -- its bit must be back to hidden in this stage.

stages.name: The journey underway
stages.desc: Oak's Lab story is complete and the Poke Balls are in hand. The watcher on value 5 has fired its two one-shots: the after-Poke-Balls flags are set and Daisy has left her chair to stroll (her sitting/walking sprites are Blues House objects).
stages.timeline: After Oak's Lab wraps up -- the open game begins.

trigger.text: EVENT_GOT_POKEBALLS_FROM_OAK set (Oak's Lab) plus, for Daisy, Town Map received after entering Blue's house.

saveNotes: EVENT_DAISY_WALKING also swaps Blues House's TOGGLE_DAISY_SITTING (hide) for TOGGLE_DAISY_WALKING (show) -- those bits live on Blues House's page.

OaksLab.notes: The richest script table in the game: 19 values, of which only 0, 6, 10 and 18 are stages a save meaningfully rests in. The story is one long line -- follow Oak in, choose, be challenged, battle, then (after the parcel run) the Pokedex. Which starter balls vanish depends on the player's and rival's choices; the script computes the indices at runtime, so this blueprint flags them in words rather than guessing bits.

stages.name: The lab, waiting
stages.desc: Blue paces the lab, the three starter balls sit on the table, Oak is absent (both his lab sprites hidden). Value 0 watches for EVENT_OAK_APPEARED_IN_PALLET to start the entrance cutscene.

advance.text: Enter the lab after Oak collars you in Pallet Town -- Oak walks in behind you (values 1-5) and asks you to choose a Pokemon.

stages.name: Choose your partner
stages.desc: Oak stands at his desk (OAK_1 shown), Blue waits, three balls on the table. Value 6 is the 'don't go away' watcher -- trying to leave walks you back (value 7). This is the stage a save sits in while the player dithers over the choice.

trigger.text: The follow-Oak entrance cutscene finished; Oak has asked you to pick.

advance.text: Take a ball from the table; Blue takes the counter-pick (values 8-9).

stages.name: Starters chosen -- the rival wants a word
stages.desc: Both starters are picked (EVENT_GOT_STARTER). Value 10 waits for you to head for the door, at which point Blue challenges you to the first battle of the game (values 11-12).

trigger.text: You took a ball and confirmed; Blue grabbed his.

advance.text: Walk toward the exit -- Blue stops you and the first rival battle begins.

saveNotes: WHICH two starter balls are gone depends on the choices made -- the script computes the object indices at runtime. TOGGLE_STARTER_BALL_3 hidden here is the statically-written case; set the pair that matches the party you are building.

stages.name: First rivalry settled
stages.desc: The lab battle is done (win or lose -- the flag records that it happened) and Blue has stormed out. The byte rests on NOOP (18) until the Pokedex cutscene re-arms it from outside.
stages.timeline: After the first rival battle, before the parcel run.

trigger.text: The first rival battle resolved; Blue walked out (values 13-14).

advance.text: Fetch Oak's Parcel from Viridian's mart and bring it back -- Blue is summoned and Oak hands out the Pokedexes (values 15-17).

stages.name: The Pokedex handed down
stages.desc: Parcel delivered, Pokedexes off the side table and in two trainers' pockets, and Blue gone for good. This also ARMS Route 22's first ambush (the rival-wants-battle pair set, his sprite shown there) and swaps Viridian City's grumpy old man for the caffeinated one.
stages.timeline: The end of the tutorial -- the world opens.

trigger.text: Returned to Oak carrying Oak's Parcel.

saveNotes: Cross-map: shows Route 22's TOGGLE_ROUTE_22_RIVAL_1 (his page), and swaps Viridian City's TOGGLE_LYING_OLD_MAN (hide) for TOGGLE_OLD_MAN (show).
EVENT_GOT_POKEBALLS_FROM_OAK (the five Poke Balls, later) is a separate talk once you have caught nothing extra -- Pallet Town's stage 3 carries it.

BluesHouse.notes: Two script values, both resting: 0 flips to 1 on first entry (EVENT_ENTERED_BLUES_HOUSE). The Town Map is a text handler on Daisy; her sitting/walking swap is driven by Pallet Town's watcher once the map is given AND the house has been entered.

stages.name: Never visited
stages.desc: Daisy sits at the table; the first entry has not been recorded.

advance.text: Walk in -- value 0 sets the entered flag and steps to 1 immediately.

stages.name: Visited -- Town Map still on offer
stages.desc: You have been inside; Daisy still has the Town Map to give.

advance.text: Talk to Daisy: she hands over the Town Map.

stages.name: Town Map given -- Daisy stretches her legs
stages.desc: Map handed over. With both the map given and the house entered, Pallet Town's watcher sets EVENT_DAISY_WALKING and swaps her sprites: sitting hidden, walking shown.

trigger.text: Received the Town Map from Daisy.

ViridianCity.notes: The script byte always rests at 0 -- values 1-3 are the old man's catching-demonstration cutscene and the walk-down nudge, all per-visit. The durable state is the old man himself: lying across the path until Oak has his parcel, then standing and demonstrating. (The gym-open rumors change by badge count in dialog only.)

stages.name: The road north is blocked
stages.desc: The old man ('I've had no coffee') lies across the northern path; the demonstrating old man is hidden.

advance.text: Deliver Oak's Parcel: the Oak's Lab Pokedex scene swaps the two old-man sprites.

stages.name: Coffee acquired
stages.desc: The old man is up, and will demonstrate catching a Pokemon (the per-visit cutscene on values 1-2). The path north is clear.

PewterCity.notes: The byte always rests at 0 -- values 1-3 and 4-6 are the two per-visit escort cutscenes (the Super Nerd who marches you to the museum, the youngster who marches you to the gym), each hiding its guide for the walk and restoring him after. The durable change belongs to Pewter Gym: beating Brock hides the gym guide for good. EVENT_BOUGHT_MUSEUM_TICKET is per-visit scratch value 0 clears on leaving the museum row.

stages.name: Guides on duty
stages.desc: Both escorts stand in the streets, ready to drag an unbadged trainer where they think he should go.

advance.text: Beat Brock next door -- his post-battle script hides the gym guide.

stages.name: The Boulder Badge earned
stages.desc: The gym guide has stood down (hidden by Pewter Gym's victory script); the museum guide keeps his post.

PewterGym.notes: The standard gym shape: DEFAULT(0) / START_BATTLE(1) / END_BATTLE(2) are the shared trainer-engine trio, and the byte rests at 0 both before and after Brock -- the STAGE lives in the flags and the badge, not the byte. Beating Brock also DISARMS Route 22's first rival ambush if it is still pending (clears the pair, hides his sprite there).

stages.name: Brock waiting
stages.desc: Brock and his Jr. Trainer hold the gym; the Gym Guide stands by the door.

advance.text: Beat Brock (post-battle value 3 hands out TM34, the Boulder Badge, and the cleanup).

stages.name: Boulder Badge won
stages.desc: Brock beaten, TM34 (Bide) given, his trainer deactivated, the Gym Guide gone from the door. If Route 22's first ambush was still armed, it is disarmed here.
saveNotes: Cross-map: also hides Pewter City's TOGGLE_GYM_GUY (the youngster who walks you to the gym) and Route 22's TOGGLE_ROUTE_22_RIVAL_1, and clears EVENT_1ST_ROUTE22_RIVAL_BATTLE + EVENT_ROUTE22_RIVAL_WANTS_BATTLE (recorded as cleared here because this gym's own script resets them).

CeruleanGym.notes: Standard gym shape; rests at 0 on both sides of Misty.

stages.name: Misty waiting
stages.desc: Misty and her two trainers hold the gym.

advance.text: Beat Misty (value 3 gives TM11 and the Cascade Badge).

stages.name: Cascade Badge won
stages.desc: Misty beaten, TM11 (BubbleBeam) given, her trainers deactivated.

VermilionGym.notes: Standard gym shape. The trash-can lock puzzle is NOT event flags: the two switch positions are the global Main-Data bytes 0x29EF/0x29F0 (see reference/gym-safari-state.md), and the door state is derived from them on entry.

stages.name: Lt. Surge waiting
stages.desc: The electric locks are engaged (trash-can switches, stored as two global bytes) and Lt. Surge holds the gym with three trainers.

advance.text: Open the locks and beat Lt. Surge (value 3 gives TM24 and the Thunder Badge).

saveNotes: The lock puzzle lives at 0x29EF/0x29F0 (wFirstLockTrashCanIndex / wSecondLockTrashCanIndex), edited on the Map Storage panel's Vermilion Gym page.

stages.name: Thunder Badge won
stages.desc: Lt. Surge beaten, TM24 (Thunderbolt) given, his trainers deactivated.

stages.name: Erika waiting
stages.desc: Erika and her seven trainers hold the gym.

advance.text: Beat Erika (value 3 gives TM21 and the Rainbow Badge).

stages.name: Rainbow Badge won
stages.desc: Erika beaten, TM21 (Mega Drain) given, her trainers deactivated.

FuchsiaGym.notes: Standard gym shape (the invisible walls are map geometry, not state).

stages.name: Koga waiting
stages.desc: Koga and his six trainers hold the gym behind the invisible-wall maze.

advance.text: Beat Koga (value 3 gives TM06 and the Soul Badge).

stages.name: Soul Badge won
stages.desc: Koga beaten, TM06 (Toxic) given, his trainers deactivated.

SaffronGym.notes: Standard gym shape (the teleporter maze is map geometry). Reaching Sabrina at all presumes Silph Co is liberated only in story terms, not in code.

stages.name: Sabrina waiting
stages.desc: Sabrina and her seven trainers hold the gym behind the warp-tile maze.

advance.text: Beat Sabrina (value 3 gives TM46 and the Marsh Badge).

stages.name: Marsh Badge won
stages.desc: Sabrina beaten, TM46 (Psywave) given, her trainers deactivated.

CinnabarGym.notes: Standard gym shape with one addition: the quiz-machine gate doors are their own event flags (EVENT_CINNABAR_GYM_GATE0..6, in the event-flags section), and the 'next wrong answer' opponent is the global byte 0x2CE4 (reference/gym-safari-state.md). Value 1 (GET_OPPONENT_TEXT) is the quiz plumbing.

stages.name: Blaine waiting
stages.desc: Blaine and his seven trainers hold the gym behind the quiz doors (door state = the EVENT_CINNABAR_GYM_GATE flags).

advance.text: Beat Blaine (value 3 gives TM38 and the Volcano Badge).

saveNotes: The open/closed quiz doors are EVENT_CINNABAR_GYM_GATE0..6 -- edit them in the Event flags section; the 'wrong answer opponent' byte is 0x2CE4.

stages.name: Volcano Badge won
stages.desc: Blaine beaten, TM38 (Fire Blast) given, his trainers deactivated.

ViridianGym.notes: The last gym, and the one whose victory writes far beyond its walls: beating Giovanni gives TM27 + the Earth Badge AND arms Route 22's SECOND rival ambush (sets the pair, shows his sprite there).

stages.name: Giovanni waiting
stages.desc: Giovanni, eight trainers and the spinner maze hold the late-game gym.

advance.text: Beat Giovanni (value 3 gives TM27, the Earth Badge, and arms Route 22's second ambush).

stages.name: Earth Badge won -- the rival stirs
stages.desc: Giovanni beaten and gone from gym leadership, TM27 (Fissure) given, all eight trainers deactivated -- and the rival now waits on Route 22 for the pre-Victory-Road rematch.
stages.timeline: Eighth badge held; Victory Road next.
saveNotes: Cross-map: shows Route 22's TOGGLE_ROUTE_22_RIVAL_2 (his page carries the bit).

CeruleanCity.notes: Two independent troubles share the byte's watcher: the bridge-side rival ambush (until EVENT_BEAT_CERULEAN_RIVAL) and the backyard burglar (until EVENT_BEAT_CERULEAN_ROCKET_THIEF). Both are coord-triggered from value 0; the progression order below is the usual play order, but the two are mechanically independent -- either can be toggled alone.

stages.name: A city with troubles
stages.desc: Blue lurks near the Nugget Bridge exit (his sprite hidden until the trigger fires) and the Rocket burglar stands in the robbed house's yard. Both encounters are armed.

advance.text: Walk the trigger row north of the city: Blue appears, battles you, and leaves (values 1-3).

stages.name: Rival seen off
stages.desc: The bridge battle is done and Blue is gone; the burglar still holds the yard.

trigger.text: Beat Blue at the bridge trigger.

advance.text: Confront the burglar in the backyard (coord-triggered talk, value 4 after the battle): he hands over TM28 and flees.

stages.name: Burglar chased off
stages.desc: Both troubles resolved: TM28 (Dig) surrendered, the yard empty.
saveNotes: The two troubles are independent axes -- a save with only the burglar beaten is legal; this order is the usual play order, not a mechanical requirement.

Route22.notes: The double-ambush road. BOTH ambushes are armed from outside (first: Oak's Lab Pokedex scene; second: beating Giovanni in Viridian Gym) and DISARMED here when fought -- and the first can also be disarmed by Pewter Gym if skipped. The byte rests at 0 through four of the five stages; only after the SECOND battle does it move to 7 (NOOP), which is why the byte alone cannot tell the road's story.

stages.name: A quiet road
stages.desc: No rival anywhere; both ambush sprites hidden, no arming flags set. The road is just a road with a gate at the end.

advance.text: Oak's Lab Pokedex scene arms the first ambush.

stages.name: First ambush armed
stages.desc: Blue waits near the gate for anyone chasing the badge rumor. Stepping onto the trigger tiles springs the first battle (values 1-3).
stages.timeline: Between the Pokedex and Pewter Gym.

trigger.text: EVENT_1ST_ROUTE22_RIVAL_BATTLE + EVENT_ROUTE22_RIVAL_WANTS_BATTLE set by Oak's Lab; his sprite shown.

advance.text: Fight him on the trigger tiles -- or beat Brock first, which quietly disarms this ambush.

stages.name: First ambush sprung
stages.desc: The gate-road battle happened (win or lose -- the beat flag records the encounter resolving); the arming pair is cleared and Blue is gone.

trigger.text: The first Route 22 rival battle resolved (value 3 cleans up).

advance.text: Beat Giovanni in Viridian Gym -- the second ambush arms.

stages.name: Second ambush armed
stages.desc: With all eight badges, Blue stakes out the road one last time before Victory Road. The same trigger tiles now spring the rematch (values 4-6).

trigger.text: EVENT_2ND_ROUTE22_RIVAL_BATTLE + EVENT_ROUTE22_RIVAL_WANTS_BATTLE set by Viridian Gym; his sprite shown.

advance.text: Fight the rematch on the trigger tiles.

stages.name: Both rivalries settled
stages.desc: The rematch resolved; value 6 cleans up and parks the byte on NOOP (7) -- the only stage where the byte itself moves.

trigger.text: The second Route 22 rival battle resolved.

MtMoonB2F.notes: The fossil choice -- the game's first true branch. Taking either fossil sets its GOT flag and hides its ball; the Super Nerd then battles you (EVENT_BEAT_MT_MOON_EXIT_SUPER_NERD) and takes the OTHER fossil (value 5 hides the remaining ball). FOSSIL_1 is the Dome ball, FOSSIL_2 the Helix ball. Either branch ends with both balls gone. The four floor trainers are independent beat-bits.

stages.name: Fossils untouched
stages.desc: Both fossil balls sit on the plateau with the Super Nerd guarding his find.

advance.text: Take the Dome Fossil (FOSSIL_1): the nerd battles you and claims the Helix.
Take the Helix Fossil (FOSSIL_2): the nerd battles you and claims the Dome.

stages.name: Took the Dome Fossil
stages.desc: Dome Fossil in the bag, the Super Nerd beaten and away with the Helix; the plateau is bare.

trigger.text: Picked up the Dome Fossil, then resolved the Super Nerd battle (values 3-5 run the handover).

stages.name: Took the Helix Fossil
stages.desc: Helix Fossil in the bag, the Super Nerd beaten and away with the Dome; the plateau is bare.

trigger.text: Picked up the Helix Fossil, then resolved the Super Nerd battle.

BillsHouse.notes: Bill's teleporter accident. The byte rests at 0 in both stages -- values 1-2 are the walk-to-machine cutscene, 3 the Bill-pops-out watcher, 4-5 cleanup/PC plumbing. The S.S. Ticket is handed over in the thank-you conversation right after the separation.

stages.name: A Pokemon answers the door
stages.desc: A small Pokemon paces the floor claiming to be Bill; human Bill is nowhere to be seen. He asks you to run the Cell Separator while he climbs into the machine.

advance.text: Agree to help: the machine cutscene runs (values 1-3) and Bill is restored; talk to him for the S.S. Ticket.

stages.name: Bill restored -- ticket in hand
stages.desc: The separation worked: human Bill is at home (his Pokemon shape gone) and the S.S. Anne ticket is yours.
stages.timeline: The errand that opens Vermilion's dock.

trigger.text: Ran the Cell Separator; took the thank-you conversation.

VermilionCity.notes: The byte's own values are per-visit walk nudges (dock gate checks); the durable state of the city is whether the S.S. Anne is still in port -- a flag owned by the ship's departure cutscene, read by the dock guard here.

stages.name: The S.S. Anne in port
stages.desc: The liner is docked; with the S.S. Ticket the guard lets you down to the harbor.
stages.timeline: Between Cerulean and the Thunder Badge.

advance.text: Get HM01 from the captain and leave the ship -- it departs.

stages.name: The ship has sailed
stages.desc: The S.S. Anne has left; the dock guard turns everyone away for good.

trigger.text: EVENT_SS_ANNE_LEFT set by the departure cutscene at the dock.

SSAnne2F.notes: The rival corridor. There is NO beat-event for this battle -- the script byte itself is the record: it parks on NOOP (4) once the encounter has resolved, which is exactly why blueprints must carry the byte and not just flags.

stages.name: Rival aboard
stages.desc: Blue is somewhere on the ship; crossing the corridor trigger makes him appear (his sprite is shown by the trigger itself) and battle you.

advance.text: Cross the corridor trigger and resolve the battle (values 1-3).

stages.name: Rival dispatched
stages.desc: The corridor fight is over and Blue is gone; the byte rests on NOOP (4) -- the only durable record this encounter leaves.
stages.timeline: The S.S. Anne visit, second half.

PokemonTower2F.notes: The possessed-era rival. EVENT_POKEMON_TOWER_RIVAL_ON_LEFT is positional scratch value 0 flips per approach side; the durable facts are the beat flag and his sprite bit.

stages.name: The rival mourns
stages.desc: Blue stands on 2F and challenges you on approach -- the one battle where his team has no starter evolution question: he fights to test what the tower has done to you.

advance.text: Resolve the battle (value 1 sets the beat flag; value 2 walks him out).

stages.name: Rival beaten
stages.desc: The 2F battle is done and Blue has left the tower.

PokemonTower6F.notes: The ghost Marowak blocks the stairs until beaten. Seeing it as a Marowak (rather than an unfightable GHOST) needs the Silph Scope -- an inventory fact, not a flag. The three floor channelers are independent beat-bits.

stages.name: The ghost bars the way
stages.desc: The restless Marowak haunts the stairwell; without the Silph Scope it cannot even be fought.

advance.text: With the Silph Scope, beat the Marowak (value 4 resolves it).

stages.name: Marowak laid to rest
stages.desc: The mother Marowak is at peace; the stairs to 7F are open.
saveNotes: The three channeler trainers are independent EVENT_BEAT_POKEMONTOWER_6_TRAINER_* bits, not part of this stage's story line.

PokemonTower7F.notes: The rescue. Beating the three Rockets and talking to Mr. Fuji warps you to his house; the same script hides his tower sprite, shows him at home (TOGGLE_MR_FUJIS_HOUSE_MR_FUJI) and toggles two Saffron City townsfolk (TOGGLE_SAFFRON_CITY_E/F) -- all cross-map bits carried on their own pages.

stages.name: Rockets hold Mr. Fuji
stages.desc: Three Rocket guards and, behind them, Mr. Fuji kneeling at the top of the tower.

advance.text: Beat the three Rockets and speak to Mr. Fuji -- he takes you home (value 4).

stages.name: Mr. Fuji rescued
stages.desc: The Rockets beaten and Mr. Fuji home in his volunteer house (where the Poke Flute waits). His tower sprite is gone; the guards' sprites remain, defeated.
stages.timeline: Tower resolved; the Poke Flute next.

trigger.text: Spoke to Mr. Fuji after clearing his guards.

saveNotes: Cross-map: shows TOGGLE_MR_FUJIS_HOUSE_MR_FUJI and toggles TOGGLE_SAFFRON_CITY_E/F -- their bits live on those maps' pages. EVENT_GOT_POKE_FLUTE is Mr. Fuji's House's story.

RocketHideoutB4F.notes: Giovanni's first defeat. The elevator's Lift Key and the item balls are separate concerns; this page is the boss floor.

stages.name: Giovanni in his den
stages.desc: Giovanni waits behind two guards at the bottom of the hideout.

advance.text: Beat Giovanni (value 3): he vanishes, leaving the Silph Scope behind.

stages.name: Giovanni fled -- the Silph Scope left behind
stages.desc: Giovanni beaten and gone; where he stood, the Silph Scope ball has appeared.
stages.timeline: Hideout cleared; Lavender's tower can now be understood.

SilphCo7F.notes: The occupied tower's rival floor. Blue ambushes from the stairwell (coord-triggered from value 0). His beat flag and sprite bit are the durable pair; the four floor Rockets/scientists are independent beat-bits.

stages.name: The rival lurks by the stairs
stages.desc: Blue waits at the 7F stairwell to test you mid-liberation.

advance.text: Cross the stairwell trigger and resolve the battle (values 3-5).

stages.name: Rival beaten
stages.desc: The stairwell fight is over and Blue has left the building.

SilphCo11F.notes: The president's floor and Giovanni's second defeat -- the flag that liberates the whole building (dozens of guard/employee objects across all 11 floors read EVENT_BEAT_SILPH_CO_GIOVANNI through their filter flags).

stages.name: Giovanni holds the president
stages.desc: Giovanni stands over Silph's president with two guards on the floor.

advance.text: Beat Giovanni (values 3-5): Team Rocket abandons the building.

stages.name: Silph Co liberated
stages.desc: Giovanni beaten a second time; the occupation collapses (every floor's Rocket-vs-employee objects read this flag), and the grateful president will hand over the Master Ball.
saveNotes: Giovanni's own sprite visibility on this floor follows the beat flag through the engine's toggleable-object machinery rather than an explicit Show/Hide in this script.

Route12.notes: The sleeping Snorlax (south one). EVENT_FIGHT_ROUTE12_SNORLAX is battle-arming scratch value 0 clears; the durable pair is the beat flag + the sprite bit. Waking it needs the Poke Flute -- an inventory fact.

stages.name: Snorlax blocks the road
stages.desc: A Snorlax sleeps across the route; only the Poke Flute will rouse it.

advance.text: Play the Poke Flute beside it and resolve the battle (catch, beat, or flee -- value 3 cleans up).

stages.name: The road is clear
stages.desc: The Snorlax is gone (caught or fled); the route runs unbroken.

Route16.notes: The sleeping Snorlax (west one) -- Route 12's twin in every mechanical respect.

stages.name: Snorlax blocks Cycling Road
stages.desc: A Snorlax sleeps in front of the Cycling Road gate; only the Poke Flute will rouse it.

advance.text: Play the Poke Flute beside it and resolve the battle (value 3 cleans up).

stages.name: The way west is clear
stages.desc: The Snorlax is gone; Cycling Road is reachable.

LoreleisRoom.notes: Elite Four room one. The byte rests at 0 on both sides of the fight; the durable facts are the autowalk one-shot and the beat flag. The corridor block behind Lorelei is a DERIVED tile: recomputed on every entry from the beat flag (0x24 blocked / 0x05 clear). Entering also sets BIT_STARTED_ELITE_4 (0x29E0 bit 1) -- the flag Indigo Plateau's lobby reads to WIPE an unfinished run (see reference/world-completed.md).

stages.name: The challenge begins
stages.desc: Lorelei waits; the door behind her is sealed until she falls. On first entry the game autowalks you six steps in and won't let you back out.

advance.text: Beat Lorelei.

saveNotes: Entering sets the startedElite4 bit (0x29E0 b1); Indigo Plateau Lobby reads it to reset an abandoned run's E4 flags.

stages.name: Lorelei defeated
stages.desc: The ice specialist is beaten; the corridor to Bruno stands open (the exit block is re-derived from this flag on every entry).

BrunosRoom.notes: Elite Four room two -- Lorelei's shape exactly.

stages.name: The challenge continues
stages.desc: Bruno waits; the door behind him is sealed until he falls.

advance.text: Beat Bruno.

stages.name: Bruno defeated
stages.desc: The fighting master is beaten; the corridor to Agatha stands open (derived from this flag on entry).

AgathasRoom.notes: Elite Four room three -- the same shape again.

stages.name: The challenge deepens
stages.desc: Agatha waits; the door behind her is sealed until she falls.

advance.text: Beat Agatha.

stages.name: Agatha defeated
stages.desc: The ghost veteran is beaten; the corridor to Lance stands open (derived from this flag on entry).

LancesRoom.notes: Elite Four room four. Beating Lance sets EVENT_BEAT_LANCE (his talk handler) alongside the trainer beat-bit; EVENT_LANCES_ROOM_LOCK_DOOR is the door-seal one-shot behind you.

stages.name: The dragon master waits
stages.desc: Lance, last of the four. The door seals behind you as you approach him.

advance.text: Beat Lance.

stages.name: Lance defeated
stages.desc: The Elite Four are beaten -- and Lance reveals someone got here first. The way to the Champion's room is open.
stages.timeline: Four of four; one battle left.

ChampionsRoom.notes: The final battle. Value 0 launches the confrontation cutscene the moment you walk in; everything through value 10 is that one scene (battle, Oak's arrival, the walk to the Hall of Fame). The single durable fact here is EVENT_BEAT_CHAMPION_RIVAL; the Hall of Fame map handles the induction and the E4-flag wipe.

stages.name: The Champion is... Blue
stages.desc: Your rival took the throne minutes ago. Walking in starts the final battle sequence; Oak's congratulation sprite is hidden until his entrance.
stages.timeline: The last door of the game.

advance.text: Beat the Champion; Oak arrives, and you are walked to the Hall of Fame (values 1-10).

stages.name: Champion dethroned
stages.desc: Blue's reign is over. The full ending cutscene has played and Oak has come and gone (his sprite hidden again).
saveNotes: The Hall of Fame's own script performs the induction, hides Cerulean's cave guard, and RESETS the ten Elite-Four run flags -- see the Hall of Fame page.

HallOfFame.notes: The induction machine. Value 1 runs Oak's congratulations and hides Cerulean City's cave guard (cross-map); value 2 RESETS the whole Elite-Four run (all ten flags -- door one-shots, beat flags, the champion flag) so the gauntlet re-arms, then saves the game. That wipe is why a post-game save shows the E4 'unbeaten': the run flags are cleared BY DESIGN, and only the Hall of Fame records remain.

stages.name: Awaiting an inductee
stages.desc: The recording room, idle.
stages.timeline: Reached only at the story's end.

advance.text: Arrive with Oak after beating the Champion: induction, flag wipe, save (values 1-2).

stages.name: Inducted -- the gauntlet re-armed
stages.desc: Your team is in the Hall of Fame and the byte rests on NOOP (3). Every Elite-Four run flag has been deliberately cleared so the four rooms and the Champion are fightable again.

trigger.text: The induction ran to completion (it saves the game itself).

saveNotes: Cross-map: hides Cerulean City's TOGGLE_CERULEAN_CAVE_GUY -- Cerulean Cave opens.

The ten E4 run flags (EVENT_AUTOWALKED_INTO_*, EVENT_BEAT_*S_ROOM_TRAINER_0, EVENT_BEAT_LANCE, EVENT_BEAT_CHAMPION_RIVAL, EVENT_LANCES_ROOM_LOCK_DOOR) read CLEARED here on purpose -- that is the machine working, not a missing record.

SafariZoneGate.notes: Per-visit machinery, deliberately ONE stage: the byte's values are the join/leave conversation and walk nudges, EVENT_IN_SAFARI_ZONE flips per visit, and the step/ball counters are the global Safari bytes (0x29B9/0x2CF3, re-seeded at the gate each game). Nothing here is a story progression.

stages.name: The gate, between games
stages.desc: The reception desk in its resting shape. Everything this map writes (the in-Safari flag, the walk nudges, the counters) is per-visit machinery re-armed each time -- there is no later stage to roll to.
saveNotes: Safari steps-left (0x29B9, big-endian) and balls (0x2CF3) live on the Map Storage panel's Safari Zone page.
